item.title: How fast is 10 tokens per second really? 摘要
item.description:
Mike Veerman created a simple HTML app that simulates LLM token output speeds ranging from 5 to 800 tokens per second. The tool helps users visualize what different token rates look like in practice, making it easier to understand advertised speeds like '30 tokens/second'. The source code is available on GitHub.
标签: AI, LLM, token speed, simulation, visualization, developer tools, generative AI, performance

item.title: ‘You Do Not Need Fancy Equipment, You Do Not Need a Degree, to Make Money and to Do This as Your Job’ 摘要
item.description:
Pop singer-songwriter Brye reveals that her hit song 'Lemons,' which went viral in 2020 and has been streamed over 100 million times, was produced entirely on GarageBand using a school-issued iPad. She recorded it with a cheap plug-in microphone and posted it to spite someone. Brye emphasizes that expensive equipment or a degree is unnecessary to succeed in music, as GarageBand is free on Apple devices. Her story exemplifies Apple's mission to empower creativity with accessible tools.
标签: GarageBand, iPad, music production, viral hit, DIY music, Apple, creativity, indie artist, TikTok, success story

item.title: NYT: ‘Powered by A.I., Google Changes Its Search Box for the First Time in 25 Years’ 摘要
item.description:
Google I/O 2026 keynote announced the first redesign of Google's search bar since 2001, making it larger and more interactive to support longer queries, images, and videos. The update also introduces chatbot follow-ups and AI agents that automate searches like apartment listings, potentially bypassing sites like Zillow. While Google frames this as user convenience, analysts warn it accelerates the decline of the open web, reducing other sites to raw data providers. Google's ad revenue and profit have surged, with annual profit doubling to $132 billion since 2022.
标签: Google, AI, search, Google I/O, open web, advertising, technology, user experience, data

item.title: Quoting SpaceX S-1 摘要
item.description:
SpaceX disclosed in an SEC filing that it entered into cloud services agreements with Anthropic in May 2026, providing compute capacity from its COLOSSUS and COLOSSUS II data centers. Anthropic will pay $1.25 billion per month through May 2029, with reduced fees during the ramp-up period. The agreements can be terminated by either party with 90 days' notice. SpaceX also uses these compute resources for its own AI applications, including training Grok 5 at COLOSSUS II.
标签: SpaceX, Anthropic, cloud computing, AI, Grok, COLOSSUS, SEC filing, compute capacity, data center, partnership

item.title: Read Cindy Cohn's new book, Privacy's Defender: My Thirty-Year Fight Against Digital Surveillance 摘要
item.description:
Cindy Cohn's memoir 'Privacy's Defender: My Thirty-Year Fight Against Digital Surveillance' recounts three major legal battles: freeing cryptography from government control in the 1990s (establishing code as speech), challenging NSA mass surveillance after whistleblower Mark Klein's revelations and Edward Snowden's leaks, and fighting against unaccountable National Security Letters with gag orders. The book interweaves personal stories with legal history. EFF technologist Micah Lee, who helped connect Snowden with journalists, is mentioned for his role in the NSA spying case.
标签: privacy, surveillance, cryptography, NSA, EFF, digital rights, memoir, legal, whistleblower, First Amendment
